commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `--safe-formula` and `--verify` to `gog sheets update`, reading single formulas from files and optionally reading the updated range back for spreadsheet error tokens.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR. Source inspection shows current main already supports file/stdin JSON input through `--values-json`, but no live Google Sheets behavior was demonstrated for the new flags.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer direction on the overlapping Sheets update flags before any merge path.

target.title: feat(sheets): add --safe-formula and --verify flags to update command
target.body:
## Summary

- **`--safe-formula <file>`** — reads a formula from a file path instead of inline, avoiding shell `!` expansion that corrupts cross-tab references like `=IMPORTRANGE!` or `='Other Tab'!C9`. Automatically forces `USER_ENTERED` mode.
- **`--verify`** — after any write, reads the updated range back and warns if the computed value contains a spreadsheet error token (`#ERROR!`, `#REF!`, `#NAME?`, `#VALUE!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#N/A`, `#NUM!`). Works independently of `--safe-formula`.

## Usage

```bash
# Write a formula safely from a file
echo "=B5-B6-'Other Tab'!C9" > /tmp/formula.txt
gog sheets update <sheetId> "Sheet1!B13" --safe-formula /tmp/formula.txt

# Verify the write didn't produce a spreadsheet error
gog sheets update <sheetId> "Sheet1!B13" --safe-formula /tmp/formula.txt --verify

# --verify works with any write, not just --safe-formula
gog sheets update <sheetId> "Sheet1!B13" --values-json '[["=SUM(A1:A10)"]]' --verify
```

## Notes

- `--safe-formula` is mutually exclusive with `--values-json` and positional values (first match wins in the switch)
- `--verify` exits cleanly if the updated range is empty (e.g. dry-run mode)
- Uses the existing `sheetsDefaultValueInputOption` constant for consistency

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: alexknowshtml
